candidates.direction_name: Retune PTX Hot-Band Grouped Rows From 4 Down To 2 On The 3-Stage Surface
ranking_notes: This is the cheapest single-variable probe that targets the exact regressions introduced by the 3-stage run: barrier and mio_throttle.
It keeps the long_scoreboard win from the 3-stage surface instead of throwing that information away immediately.
If it loses, the result cleanly tells us whether the remaining tax is in CTA group depth or in the late-drain seam itself.
candidates.hypothesis: The current 3-stage run already removed long_scoreboard as the dominant tax, so the remaining regression is mostly handoff depth: barrier is up by 1.25 pts and mio_throttle is up by 0.59 while active warps stay flat. Reducing PTX grouped rows from 4 to 2 is the smallest way to shorten CTA group depth and reduce synchronization pressure without giving back the 3-stage latency-hiding benefit or touching the active PTX dispatch.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Grouped-row batching is likely over-amortizing locality on the 3-stage surface and paying extra barrier plus handoff delay per CTA group.
candidates.risk: Low-moderate. This is a one-constant launch-order retune on the current winning kernel body, but smaller CTA groups can still lose locality if the batching cut is too aggressive.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if grouped_rows=2 materially increases DRAM or L2 pressure without reducing barrier or mio_throttle, or if it forces a broader dispatch-family rewrite.
expected_local_changes: Do not touch launch bounds, stage count, or the accumulator schedule in the first pass.

candidates.direction_name: Split The Final 3-Stage PTX Drain Out Of The Late Steady-State Loop
ranking_notes: This is the next-smallest sync cleanup after grouped_rows and stays entirely on the current 3-stage PTX surface.
It is ranked behind dir_01 because grouped_rows=2 is a simpler probe with cleaner attribution if it wins or loses.
The idea still matters because the 3-stage run proved the latency-side benefit is real, so the next job is to shrink the coordination tail.
candidates.hypothesis: The 3-stage ring now waits with thresholds 2 -> 1 -> 0 as the pipeline drains, but the late steady-state path still pays a full CTA sync on every remaining handoff. Pulling the no-refill drain into a narrower late-loop path should preserve the long_scoreboard win while trimming the extra barrier and mio_throttle cost that appeared after the 3-stage expansion.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Late-drain synchronization is now the most likely remaining local tax on the 3-stage surface.
candidates.risk: Moderate. This stays within the current PTX kernel body, but it touches performance-sensitive drain logic and can still break correctness if the final wait/sync thresholds are mistimed.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the late-drain split requires another stage-count change, a launch rewrite, or wider hot-loop predicates than a tiny late-path branch.
expected_local_changes: Split the late no-refill drain from the current 3-stage steady-state path.
Keep stage count at 3 and leave grouped_rows unchanged in the first pass.
Do not touch launch bounds or the accumulator schedule while testing the drain seam.

candidates.direction_name: Reopen 256x128 64x64-Warp Hot-Band Tiling On The Dominant Surface
ranking_notes: This keeps the explicit 256x128 / 64x64 guidance alive without pretending it is the first response to a surface that just produced a clear local lesson.
It is still the highest-ceiling structural branch left if the current 128x128 PTX family stalls again.
Rank 3 is intentional: the 3-stage probe already found a real win in long_scoreboard, so one more narrow local fix should come first.
candidates.hypothesis: The current PTX surface is still above both the accepted base and the best known custom run, and it remains capped at 16.60% active warps even after the 3-stage overlap fix. The source already carries a 256x128 hot-band path, and the persistent human guidance still asks for 256x128 block tiling with 64x64 warp tiling plus Right-Left-Right-Left reuse. That structural branch should stay alive as the next high-ceiling option if one more narrow local probe fails.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: The 128x128 PTX hot-band surface may be hitting a real tiling and reuse ceiling even after local pipeline cleanup.
candidates.risk: High. This is a broader geometry reopen with known correctness and performance history, and it touches launch routing, writer ownership, and export layout together.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness on all 3 dataset cases before trusting runtime
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the reopen requires replaying a previously broken ownership or export path instead of a writer-safe 256x128 surface.
expected_local_changes: Reopen the fixed hot-band launch routing for the 256x128 dominant surface.
Keep writer ownership explicit and export-safe.
Preserve the 64x64 warp-tile reuse pattern instead of reviving the broken half-panel writer split.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
